key_insight: Chat-ISV uses a knowledge graph plus multi-agent retrieval to give traceable, lower-hallucination answers for hard industrial VOCs questions in the steel industry.
engineering_relevance: It turns scattered environmental engineering literature into a decision-support system that can help engineers identify and compare pollution-control options with source-backed evidence.
method: Parse a curated steel-industry VOCs literature corpus into a Neo4j knowledge graph.
Use prompt-constrained extraction, chunk-centered topology optimization, and source-backtracking retrieval.
Combine multi-agent routing, local literature retrieval, open-domain knowledge access, and subgraph visualization.
evidence_signal: The system was built on 27,180 nodes and 81,779 semantic edges, and evaluation showed 96.93% precision, 72.63% recall, an F1-score of 0.830, and a mean expert score of 1.69/2.00.
watch_item: Performance depends on the quality and coverage of the curated literature corpus, so incomplete or outdated sources could limit answer reliability.
paper_title: Traceable Knowledge Graph Reasoning Enables LLM-Assisted Decision Support for Industrial VOCs in the Steel Industry
